article_title: Nvidia Posts Record $81.6 Billion Q1 Revenue, Raises Quarterly Dividend 25x and Authorizes $80 Billion Buyback
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Nvidia Posts Record $81.6 Billion Q1 Revenue, Raises Quarterly Dividend 25x and Authorizes $80 Billion Buyback
content_preview.summary: Nvidia's Q1 FY2027 results set new company records across revenue, net income, and free cash flow, driven by Blackwell GPU demand and a near-tripling of networking revenue.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nvidia reported record first-quarter fiscal 2027 revenue of $81.6 billion on May 20, 2026, up 85 percent year over year and 20 percent sequentially, according to [NVIDIA's official press release](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-first-quarter-fiscal-2027). The results beat Wall Street expectations by roughly $2.8 billion, and the company guided for $91.0 billion in second-quarter revenue — a figure that itself exceeded prior analyst consensus...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Briefing covering Nvidia Q1 FY2027 earnings. All four sections (Revenue by Segment, Profitability, Blackwell Demand, Capital Returns, Q2 Guidance) are logically ordered and appropriately detailed for the Briefing category. Word count (980) is within the 300-800 Briefing range. Article correctly flags the China guidance exclusion and links to prior coverage of the H200 story.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four snapshots fetched successfully with HTTP 200. Source-0 (nvidianews.nvidia.com, official press release): Confirms all headline figures — $81.6B revenue, 85% YoY, 20% seq, $75.2B data center, $60.4B compute, $14.8B networking, 199% YoY networking, 35% seq, GAAP gross margin 74.9%, non-GAAP 75.0%, GAAP net income $58.3B, operating income $53.5B, GAAP diluted EPS $2.39 (214% YoY), $20B shareholder returns, $38.5B remaining buyback, $80B new buyback, dividend raised from $0.01 to $0.25, Edge Computing $6.4B 29% YoY, Q2 guidance $91B ±2%, China exclusion confirmed, free cash flow $48,554M ($48.6B). Source-1 (stocktitan.net): Confirms main Q1 figures, operating cash flow $50.3B, dividend and buyback figures. Two claims attributed to this source are NOT present in the snapshot: (a) the Hyperscale segment figure of $37.9B up 115% YoY and ACIE segment $37.4B up 74% YoY; and (b) 'free cash flow $48.6 billion up 86 percent' — the FCF figure is in the press release but not stated explicitly in the StockTitan snapshot. The Hyperscale/ACIE specifics appear to originate from Nvidia's CFO commentary PDF (investor.nvidia.com), which is not cited. Source-2 (futurumgroup.com): Confirms CFO Kress 'fastest product ramp' quote, Jensen Huang 'Compute is revenues. Compute is profit' quote, AWS one-million GPU commitment, Blackwell Ultra 2.7x throughput and 60% cost-per-token reduction, sovereign AI 40 countries / 80%+ YoY growth, ACIE 31% sequential growth, $13.5B sequential revenue increase as company record. One claim attributed to this source is NOT in the snapshot: the '52 percent year over year' operating expense increase — this figure appears in the Quiver Quantitative source and the press release, not in Futurum. Source-3 (quiverquant.com): Confirms Jensen Huang 'uniquely positioned' quote verbatim, gross margins 74.9%/75.0%, China exclusion in Q2 guidance, $80B buyback. The combined $118.5B open buyback figure attributed to this source is not stated explicitly but is accurately derivable from figures that ARE in the source ($38.5B remaining + $80B new). Two sources (futurumgroup.com and quiverquant.com) are not on the source allowlist, as flagged by automated checks — both are credible secondary sources (analyst firm and financial data platform) that accurately relay the Nvidia press release.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All headline claims are accurate and verified against the Nvidia press release (source-0). Two source attribution errors found in the body: (1) Hyperscale segment $37.9B/115% YoY and ACIE $37.4B/74% YoY figures are attributed to StockTitan but not present in that snapshot — these likely originate from Nvidia's CFO commentary (uncited). The underlying figures appear internally consistent with total DC revenue of $75.2B; (2) Operating expenses 52% YoY increase is attributed to Futurum Group but is not present in that snapshot — the figure is confirmed accurate via the press release and Quiver Quantitative. Both errors are body-level misattributions, not fabrications. The core facts are correct and verifiable from the primary source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no loaded language, no AI self-reference. The Analysis section appropriately frames the scale of the results and the China risk without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found. Existing Nvidia articles in May 2026 cover the H200 China sales clearance (May 16), Vera CPU deliveries (May 21), and ARM laptop chip preview (May 22) — all distinct topics. This is the first article covering Q1 FY2027 earnings.
concerns: Hyperscale ($37.9B, 115% YoY) and ACIE ($37.4B, 74% YoY) segment figures are attributed to StockTitan but are not in the StockTitan snapshot. These figures appear to derive from Nvidia's CFO commentary PDF (investor.nvidia.com), which is not among the cited sources.

The 52% operating expense year-over-year increase in the 'What We Don't Know' section is attributed to Futurum Group but is not present in the Futurum snapshot — the figure is accurate per the press release and Quiver Quantitative.
futurumgroup.com and quiverquant.com are not on the source allowlist, though both are credible secondary outlets.
recommendations: File a corrections record noting the two body-level attribution mismatches: (a) Hyperscale/ACIE segment figures attributed to StockTitan vs. their actual origin in the CFO commentary; (b) 52% OpEx increase attributed to Futurum vs. its actual appearance in the press release/Quiver.
Consider adding futurumgroup.com and quiverquant.com to config/source_allowlist.txt given their demonstrated accuracy on financial reporting.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate Briefing on a significant earnings event. All core facts are correct and verified against the Nvidia press release. Two body-level source attribution mismatches are recoverable through a corrections record. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
